description: Audit the codebase: identify all Chrome headless dependencies, usages, and any headless browser integration points across all Cargo.toml files and source files

Research and select the appropriate Qt Rust wrapper for WebKit (e.g. qmetaobject-rs, qt-rust, or ritual-generated bindings) — confirm latest Qt6 + WebKit compatibility and crate availability on crates.io
Replace the LICENSE file content with the full LGPL-3.0 license text
Update the license field in every Cargo.toml (workspace root, cli/, core/, worker/) from 'MIT' to 'LGPL-3.0'
Remove all Chrome/headless-browser crate dependencies (e.g. headless_chrome, chromiumoxide, fantoccini) from Cargo.toml files
Add the selected Qt WebKit Rust wrapper crate(s) to the appropriate Cargo.toml(s) with the latest compatible versions
Replace Chrome headless browser initialization and page-loading code with equivalent Qt WebKit API calls (QWebEngineView or WebKitWebView), preserving all existing public interfaces
Replace Chrome headless screenshot, DOM query, and JavaScript evaluation calls with the corresponding Qt WebKit equivalents
Update README.md, IMPLEMENTATION.md, and any inline code comments to reflect the WebKit/Qt stack and LGPL-3.0 license
Run the full build and test suite (cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace) and fix any compilation or test failures introduced by the browser engine swap
Verify the Dockerfile in action/ correctly installs Qt6 + WebKit system libraries required at runtime and update it if needed
Run cargo clippy --workspace and cargo fmt --check; resolve any new warnings or formatting issues
